'''Jordan Walne''' (born 28 December 1992) is an English professional  [[rugby league]] footballer who plays in the {{rlp|sr}} for the [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He has previously played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]], plus [[Oldham RLFC]] on dual-registration.

==Background==
He is the [[brother]] of fellow [[rugby league]] footballer, [[Adam Walne]]. As an amateur he played for the Leyland Warriors, based at Malt Kiln Fold, [[Leyland, Lancashire|Leyland]].

==Senior career==

===Salford Red Devils (2013-17)===
Walne made his senior début for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] on 21 April 2013, in a [[Challenge Cup]] match against the [[Gloucestershire All Golds]]. Walne made his [[Super League]] début on 4 August 2013, in the [[Salford Red Devils]]' fixture against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].

===Hull Kingston Rovers (2018)===
In October 2017, Walne signed a one-year deal to play for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41748395|title=Tommy Lee and Jordan Walne: Hull KR sign St Helens hooker and Salford prop|publisher=BBC Sport|date=25 October 2017|accessdate=8 December 2017}}</ref>
It was revealed on 10 October 2018, that Walne would be departing [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] following a restructure of the club's on field personnel.<ref>{{cite web|date=10 October 2018|title=Mass exodus at Hull Kingston Rovers|url=http://mobile.twitter.com/hullkrofficial/status/1050083609120567298}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions (2018)===
It was revealed on 26 July 2018, that Walne would spend the remainder of the 2018 [[rugby league]] season at [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]]. On a loan basis from his parent-club [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].

Walne was followed to [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] club by two of his current [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] teammates in [[Will Dagger]] and [[Josh Johnson (rugby league)|Josh Johnson]] as part of the same loan deal. Walne made his [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] début against the [[Sheffield Eagles]] on 29 July 2018, in a 34-10 triumph in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] league competition.

===Barrow Raiders (2019-22)===
Ahead of the start of the 2019 [[rugby league]] season, Walne signed a one-year contract to play for the [[Barrow Raiders]]. Walne made his début for the Barrow Raiders on 3 February 2019, in an 18-22 victory over [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]]. Walne scored his first try for the Barrow Raiders on 24 March 2019, during a 26-33 defeat by [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]]. Walne left Barrow Raiders in February 2022.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.barrowrlfc.com/latest-news/adam-and-jordan-walne-depart-from-the-raiders/ |title=Adam and Jordan Walne depart Barrow Raiders |website=Barrow Raiders |date=28 February 2022 |access-date=6 June 2024}}</ref>

===Dual-registration===
Walne has featured on dual-registration for several clubs, including, [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] (two separate registrations, 2013 and 2017), [[Workington Town]], [[North Wales Crusaders]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.]] and the [[York City Knights]].{{citation needed|date=March 2020}}
